concepts.title: Face Value and Issue Price
concepts.description: Face value of a share is the value at which the company issues shares to the public, whereas issue price is the actual amount at which the shares are issued to the public. The issue price may be at a premium or discount to the face value.

concepts.title: Types of Share Capital
concepts.description: There are two types of share capital - Authorized share capital and Issued share capital. Authorized share capital is the maximum amount of share capital that a company is authorized to raise, while Issued share capital is the actual amount of share capital issued by the company to the public.

concepts.title: Share Capital Statement
concepts.description: A Share Capital Statement is a statement that shows the authorized, issued, subscribed, and paid-up capital of a company. It is an essential tool for analyzing the financial position of a company.

concepts.title: Hierarchy of Share Capital
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'AISAIP' to remember the hierarchy of share capital: Authorized > Issued > Subscribed > Allotted > Issued to Public.

concepts.title: Difference between Issued and Paid-up Capital
concepts.description: Many students mistake issued capital with paid-up capital. Issued capital refers to the total number of shares issued by the company, whereas paid-up capital refers to the actual amount received by the company from the shareholders towards the issued shares.

concepts.title: Components of Share Capital
concepts.description: Students must remember that share capital has three main components - Share capital, Share premium, and Reserves and Surplus. Share capital represents the actual amount received by the company, Share premium represents the amount received in excess of the face value, and Reserves and Surplus represent the retained earnings of the company.

concepts.title: Share Warrants
concepts.description: A share warrant is a document that gives the holder the option to purchase a certain number of shares at a predetermined price. Share warrants are issued by companies to their employees, debenture holders, or as a reward to customers.

concepts.title: Redemption of Debentures and Share Capital
concepts.description: When a company redeems its debentures, it must also redeem the share capital issued against those debentures. This is done to maintain the proportion of debentures and share capital in the company's capital structure.
